variations.compliment: Project Domesday looks like a great company - naming it after the 1086 Domesday Book is a clever touch given you're building the definitive record of who owns and controls what.
variations.email:
Hello Joe,

Thanks for checking out Polymer! Project Domesday looks like a great company - naming it after the 1086 Domesday Book is a clever touch given you're building the definitive record of who owns and controls what.

Happy to answer any questions you might have about Polymer.

Cheers,
Jessica

tells: naming it after the 1086 Domesday Book is a clever touch given you're building the definitive record of who owns and controls what — the trailing clause over-explains the connection that's already implied

variations.compliment: Project Domesday looks really interesting - naming it after the Domesday Book is clever, and I love that when control shifts through intermediaries the updates just propagate so teams can catch issues before they become a finding.

variations.compliment: Project Domesday looks like a really clever idea - I love that the name nods to the Domesday Book, and the way the graph propagates updates when control shifts through intermediaries seems like a smart way to catch problems before they become findings.
